Text:
Location: 11 Hereford Lane, Ararimu
Sale type: $1,999,000
Agents: Scott McElhinney : 027210 8887 | Cindy Guo : 021028 96198
URL: https://www.barfoot.co.nz/property/residential/franklin-district/ararimu/lifestyle/921877

Description:
FLAIR TO SPARE - CINEMA, POOL HOUSE + EXTRAS

HIGHLY MOTIVATED SELLERS - READY TO RELOCATE

Quality built by Build with Titus less than four years ago, this 240 square metre (approx) Axon Panel and Cedar constructed home is located on 3,124 square metres with a flat to contoured gradient. Recently installed electric gates open to a concrete driveway leading to a spectacular architectural home. Its sleek lines and refined form create a refreshingly unique residence where effortless sophistication flows from room to room, spilling outdoors to resort-style entertaining. Covered decks and open air patios wrap around the family living areas and master suite, connecting seamlessly with the heated, north facing mineral pool and its expansive alfresco surrounds.

The extras are all here, feature LED lighting, Low-E double glazing, pitched and 2.55m ceilings, data cabling, under-floor heating in the bathrooms. Attractive light-toned timber floors grace the high traffic areas including the exemplary open plan kitchen, an entertainer's delight with engineered stone benchtops, glass splash-backs, waterfall bench and breakfast bar, abundant soft close cupboards and drawers, a 900mm Smeg stove and an excellently out-fitted walk-in pantry. To one side an inviting bar stands ready to serve while behind the kitchen a luxurious media room with a hidden door is the perfect place to relax.

Picture windows, sliding doors, and bi-folds flood the adjoining dining and family living areas with natural light. A sun-filled glass conservatory off the living area provides an inviting space to relax and enjoy the glorious countryside views, while also opening out to picturesque dining and supreme entertaining. Making its own artistic statement, the wood storage alongside the full-height built-in fire is both attractive and functional.

Four bedrooms include the sun-drenched master with a window wall opening out to one of the numerous decks and those tranquil views. This incorporates an ensuite with wall-hung vanity, under-floor heating, a super-sized shower and an outstanding walk-in robe with make-up station. The further three light-filled bedrooms share a large family bathroom, also with under-floor heating and wall-hung vanity, plus a glass + tile shower and a separate bath. There's a spacious office, a separate powder room with underfloor heating and a laundry with built-ins which shares the large double lock up garage.

Outdoors, the pool house includes a bathroom, storage, and a rumpus or studio room, providing versatile additional space. There’s also a wood shed, a pump shed, and a garden shed positioned beside the fully fenced heated mineral pool with its stylish deck and patio recreational surrounds. The perfect relaxation destination! With flair to spare this indoor-outdoor exhibitionist complete with resort amenities has it all!

Title: 1186 Ararimu Road, Ararimu
Text:
Location: 1186 Ararimu Road, Ararimu
Sale type: For sale by negotiation
Agents: Lea-Ann Higgie : 021497 517
URL: https://www.barfoot.co.nz/property/residential/franklin-district/ararimu/lifestyle/923812

Description:
Charming 1920’s Bungalow on approx. 5.2ha

Enter through the tree lined drive to arrive at this beautiful character property.

Offering three double bedrooms and one single. There is a large separate lounge with woodburner
fireplace for cosy winter evenings. Modern kitchen with a trendy copper splashback combines
with the dining area and is the hub of the home. With french doors opening onto a private
back patio, you can enjoy entertaining whilst looking over the land and vista beyond.

Separate laundry and spacious bathroom plus an extra powder room.
There are many beautiful features throughout including antique light fittings and polished
timber floors.

Outside there is a double garage and workshop, plus a barn that fits a small horse truck or
horse float or can be used for a motorhome, caravan or boat. There is also a three bay
American barn that at present is set up with two horseboxes. Plenty of space here for farm
implements as well. Also a wash down bay making this property well set up for those with
horses, however lends itself to beef or most other livestock just as well.

Plenty of room on the back lawn for the kids and pets with fruit trees such as nectarine,
peach, lemon, mandarin,lime, orange, apple and guava for your summer produce.

Large yards make for ease of handling animals. The property is 5.2ha (more or less) set up
into 8 paddocks with a pond which feeds the farm troughs with water. There is also a 50,000
ltr house tank and a tank off the barn so plenty of water. The pond is fed via a spring all year
round, planted up with natives and fenced for protection.

Only a few minutes from the local Ararimu Primary school, bus to Pukekohe High School
and close to the Hunua Ranges for walking, biking or hiking.

Don’t miss seeing this one, it’s well worth a look!

Title: 1843B East Coast Road, Kaiaua
Text:
Location: 1843B East Coast Road, Kaiaua
Sale type: $1,190,000
Agents: Stephanie Alderlieste : 027246 7911
URL: https://www.barfoot.co.nz/property/residential/franklin-district/kaiaua/lifestyle/906714

Description:
Spectacular Elevated Sea Views!

Sitting snuggly amongst delightfully landscaped gardens of over an acre this solidly built brick and plaster home has gorgeous elevated rural and sea views.

You can enjoy sitting all year round on sheltered decks and watch the boaties bring home fish for tea. Or relax over a leisurely breakfast watching the sunrise over the Coromandel Ranges and the Firth of Thames!

This three bedroom home has a bright and pleasant entry hall which leads into a sunny open plan living area. Plenty of space to lounge and open the doors out to amazing decks with delightful vistas. There is a spacious kitchen with modern appliances and the lots of cupboards and work tops. A large dining area adjacent to the kitchen also has access to outdoor living with its covered arch gola style shelter and drop down siders this is a great alternate spot for Summer or Winter entertaining.
The master suite has its own ensuite and walk in wardrobe and there are two further bedrooms along the hall with a family bathroom and separate toilet. There is a double internal garage. Adjacent to the home is a large garage which is fully lined and is set up and used as Air BnB accommodation. Attracting rave reviews from all who stay here. This could also be a great workshop or hobby room.

Another 3 bay shed to the rear of the property has high stud doors giving possibilities for storing boat, camper or all the other things you might need living so near the beach. Tucked away is a further two bay open barn style shed with room for a boat.

There are many fruit trees including plums, peaches, apricots, apples, oranges, lemons, loquats amongst others and a productive vegie garden. There is plenty of room for a pet or two and kids to play!

A short stroll from the gated entrance will bring you to the most delightful little bay from where locals launch their boats and kayaks with a few locals even catching snapper from the shore.

There is a great outdoors to explore nearby with Waharau Regional Park and its wonderful bush walks, swimming off the beach just a few minutes’ walk away and great fishing spots.

Auckland is about an hour and other towns like Pukekohe and Thames are about 40 minutes away. There are great local communities in the seaside villages with the Kaiaua Primary school just down the road and a local bus to Hauraki Plains College. This is a wonderful place to retire to or for a family to live.

Kaiaua boasts a busy local boating club, plenty of fishing competitions and Sunday markets. A handy garage with full mechanic workshop, two shops and a friendly local pub with family restaurant and garden bar.

If privacy, beautiful views, mature gardens, the beach just a short stroll away seem tempting, this property is a must to view.

Description:
FUTURE URBAN ZONED - HIGH GROWTH AREA

FLEXIBLE SETTLEMENT TERMS AVAILABLE

After many years of being held in the same family this financial growth opportunity is for sale. This brilliant property has elevated views across Pukekohe township and a gentle to rolling contour. It has approximately 325 metres of road frontage along Pukekohe East Road and comprises a total of 39.26 Hectares split between Auckland and Waikato Council. It has a 360 square metre house along with multiple other outbuildings. Location is exceptional, with this site positioned 1.5km from Pukekohe, 50km south of Auckland City, 36km south of the Auckland International Airport and 4km from northern and southern motorway access at Bombay. There is an uncovenanted stand of picturesque native bush, allowing an array of birdlife to flourish.

The Auckland Council portion is 22.05 Hectares Zoned Future Urban while the remaining 17.21 Hectares is zoned Rural under Waikato Regional Council. The Future Urban Zone is a transitional zone applied to greenfield land that has been identified as suitable for urbanisation. Land may be used for a range of general rural activities but cannot be used for urban activities until the site is rezoned for urban purposes. The Pukekohe - Paerata structure Plan 2019 proposes this land to be rezoned to Residential - Mixed Housing Suburban. This plan is estimated to provide about 12,517 houses/dwellings and 5018 jobs with a population of about 33,809. As a comparison, this approximately doubles the existing population of Pukekohe.

Notable plan changes and developments in the area include:

* Anselmi Ridge is 700m to the west with current residential properties and further development underway.

* Kohe Development — 1.2km west of 140 Pukekohe East Road; 30 Hectares of Future Urban Land rezoned to Residential Mixed housing Urban.

* Proposed Private Land Change 98 — Borders the western boundary of 140 Pukekohe East Road and is proposed to rezone 27 hectares of Future Urban land to Mixed Housing Urban Zone.

* Golding Meadows and Auckland Trotting Club Development — 82 hectares re zoned to a combination of Business - Light Industry Zone (19.97ha), Residential - Mixed Housing Urban (62.35ha) and Neighbourhood Centre Zone.

Forward-thinking developers and investors will be quick to recognise the potential this property offers. Whether you land bank it while council rezones it or apply for a private plan change, the potential in this pre-programmed high-growth area is unmatched.

Surrounded by other high calibre properties and zoned Future Urban, this elevated single level 385 square metre (approx) residence resides on gently contoured 2.06 hectares (5.16 acres) and boasts a north easterly aspect with serene views across rolling countryside. Quality built by highly regarded Van Der Putten Construction in 2007 using solid brick with painted plaster and metal tile roof this home has ducted air conditioning, plus a DVS ventilation system and underfloor heating in the some living areas. Exceptional architectural appeal highlights large vehicle portico parking with impressive timber sarked ceiling, complementing feature double door entry and a soaring cathedral ceiling through the foyer and expansive open plan living area.

A beautiful tree-lined sealed driveway leads through spectacular landscaped gardens with styled hedging, tropical palms and lush swathes of lawn which wrap around this property as it steps down to the fully fenced chlorine pool with solar roller cover, in-ground spa and expansive garden-side leisure surrounds. There’s also a children’s playground here.

The entry foyer opens on two sides into breathtaking family living with an abundance of windows and a pleasing light colour palette, gleaming timber floors and an open wood fireplace with stone surround. The kitchen is superb with generous cabinetry, stone benchtop, Meile and Bosch appliances, gas hob and breakfast bar. There’s also an impressive butler’s pantry with stainless steel benchtop. Wide sliding doors in the centrally placed dining area flow out to picturesque sarked cedar-roofed entertaining, with outdoor speakers and an open patio with vine covered pergola on one side and a covered area with a skylight on the other.

In the eastern wing an office sits alongside a bathroom, there’s a bedroom - one of six – which opens to the patio and the opulent master suite with large walk-in robe, tiled bathroom with bath, shower and dual basin vanity and access to the patio overlooking the magnificent gardens and pool. There’s also a sumptuous media room with overhead projector and screen offering hours of entertainment. The western wing houses another large lounge room, four bedrooms – one opening outdoors – a laundry with storage and a spacious bathroom with large shower, dual vanity and two toilets. This bathroom is cleverly accessible from outdoors. There’s also entry off hallways end here into the double lock-up garage.

Outside, forming part of these magnificent gardens, an orchard offers lemons, grapefruit, pears and apples. There’s a garden shed, three fenced paddocks and another shed perfect for ample storage of tools, equipment, and toys. Excellent positioned in convenient proximity to schools, shops and town conveniences, this property will perfectly suit the purchaser for whom second best is just not an option.

NOTE - PART OF THIS PROPERTY IS DESIGNATED FOR THE PROPOSED PUKEKOHE NORTH EAST ARTERIAL ROAD. MORE DETAILED INFORMATION IS AVAILABLE.

SERIOUSLY FOR SALE

Our vendors’ dream home is now reluctantly offered for sale due to ill health, creating a rare chance for new owners to secure an exceptional lifestyle estate—set on 12.84ha / 32 acres (more or less) in the heart of Waiau Pa, and priced well below replacement value.

Privately located down a long driveway, this striking 440m² (more or less) home commands attention from the moment you arrive. Elevated on the highest point of the property, the home offers complete privacy and breathtaking views across farmland, with not a neighbour in sight.

Property Features:

Five year old home – built by Urban Home’s
Workshop, office and lunchroom – office, lounge/lunchroom, kitchen, laundry, double garage/storage with its own entrance
Salt water pool, heated and fully fenced
Deep water bore and water tanks
Stables – 4 oversized boxes, tack room, wash down area and undercover tie up area and loading ramp for horses
Round pen and 50m x 40m (more or less) Harrison Lane ebb and flow arena
Shedding – Truck shed L.15mxW.5mxH.8m (more or less), 4 bay implement shed L.20mxW.10mxH.6m (more or less), Stables Block and older shed by cottage
14 well fenced paddocks and cattle race and loading ramp
Security gate – two road entrance’s

The home is only five years old and presents as new. Thoughtfully designed, it features two distinct wings, providing ideal separation between the master retreat and the guest or family quarters. The master wing is a luxurious escape, with a generous bedroom, large walk-in wardrobe, and a beautifully tiled ensuite where a deep bathtub is perfectly positioned against a frame-less corner window, offering uninterrupted views while you soak in peace.

At the heart of the home is an expansive open-plan living space. The designer kitchen is made for entertaining, with a large scullery, double ovens, and a wide induction cooktop. The dining area is grand in scale, currently home to a 10-seater table, and opens through bifold doors to a covered outdoor entertaining space. The main lounge invites you to relax by the gas fireplace, while a second lounge shares that same fireplace and offers a quieter place to unwind with a book or movie. There is also the office which is cleverly designed and utilised daily by the current owners who both work from home.

The second wing of the home features three generously sized bedrooms, one with its own ensuite and the other two serviced by a sleek family bathroom. All bathrooms are fully tiled and finished to a high standard, with quality fittings that reflect the care and craftsmanship found throughout. Each space feels luxurious yet practical, designed to stand the test of time.

Behind the scenes, a dedicated server room houses the technology that powers the home’s integrated electronics and smart systems—ensuring everything from lighting to connectivity runs smoothly and efficiently. It’s a discreet yet powerful inclusion that speaks to the home’s thoughtful design and future-ready setup.

Step outside and you’ll find a setting that is just as impressive. The heated pool is a true showstopper—easily enjoyed year-round and the site of many summer celebrations. The covered entertaining area has room for a full outdoor kitchen, BBQ, and even a pizza oven, making it the perfect space for gathering with friends and family.

The land is as functional as it is picturesque. Divided into 14 well-fenced paddocks, it features a race system for efficient stock movement, loading yards, bore and a large four-bay shed ideal for machinery, storage, or workshop space. The main barn includes four oversized stables, tack room, a wash-down bay, a round pen, a truck shed plus there is a Harrison Lane ebb and flow arena. There is also another large utility shed located at the front of the property which includes good car storage along with some other old shedding — ideal for a car enthusiast, business or or additional income.

Currently enjoyed as an equine property, the possibilities here are far greater. With fertile soil, excellent infrastructure, this property could easily support horticulture, boutique farming, a market garden, or a home-based business. Whether you're dreaming of a quiet rural lifestyle, working from home in style, or running a productive land-based venture—this property is the canvas for it.

A charming Douglas Fir Log Cabin, built in the mid-1990s, sits at the heart of the property. The two-level home includes four bedrooms, two bathrooms, and open-plan living with French doors opening to a large deck and hot tub. The master bedroom also opens to its own deck with views across the land and native forest.
Currently operating as a holiday home, the cabin is approved for up to 12 guests and has run successfully for 20 years, with excellent potential for further growth. A recent renovation preserves its character while updating key areas.

Modern Four-Bedroom Home
A second dwelling, built in 2007, offers four bedrooms, two bathrooms, and open-plan living flowing to a spacious deck with views over the property.

Eco-Campground Opportunity
Approved consents allow for an eco-campground with 28 sites (16 tent, 12 powered), an office, an accessible cabin, and an amenities block with ablutions and kitchen/dining facilities. Capacity is approved for up to 100 guests.

Additional Improvements & Infrastructure
Preliminary survey and engineering advice suggests a third potential building site and positive prospects for subdivision into three river-front blocks.
The land is fully fenced for grazing. Improvements include an 80sqm half-round barn with mezzanine, power, and lighting; two 12×4m sheds/workshops with power, a farm water tank, and sheep yards.

Natural Features
The Waitawheta River forms the north-eastern boundary, offering a swimming hole, picnic area, and walking tracks. The location provides easy access to DOC tracks, fishing spots, the Hauraki Rail Trail, and popular outdoor activities.

Description:
Some Places Stay With You

For many, Slipper Island, Coromandel is not something you discover. It is something you return to. First visits often began young, arriving by boat for school camps, learning to respect the sea, sleeping with salt in the air, sharing simple meals, and spending full days on the water until the sun dipped low. Those early experiences have a way of shaping how you see the world and where you feel most alive.

Years pass and life fills with responsibility and pace, yet this coast continues to draw you back. What once felt like adventure now feels like clarity. For a rare few, there comes a moment when returning is no longer enough, and the desire shifts from visiting a place to holding it, protecting it, and making it part of your story.

What an Address
This is not just land. This is freedom, perspective, and a life lived on your own terms.

Set high above the Pacific on an expansive 4872 square metre site, 193 Pacific Ocean on Slipper Island offers a rare chance to claim your place on one of New Zealand’s most exclusive privately owned islands. A location chosen not for convenience, but for conviction.

Sweeping blue water views stretch endlessly, your own mooring sits below, and the mainland feels refreshingly distant. Access remains effortless with a short boat ride from Tairua, Pauanui and Whangamata, just an hour from Tauranga or quick helicopter flights from Auckland, Hamilton, or Tauranga.

What truly elevates this opportunity is the newly established high protection marine reserve surrounding the island. This safeguarded environment preserves the clarity of the water, the richness of marine life, and the natural balance of the coastline for generations to come. It offers reassurance that the island experience remembered from earlier years will endure, protected and respected.

The island is renowned for its sub tropical microclimate, rolling green hills, lush native vegetation, and white sand beaches that still feel untouched. Days begin with the sun rising over the ocean and end with skies painted in colour, viewed from your elevated vantage where you truly sit above it all.

Existing infrastructure allows immediate enjoyment while plans for an architectural retreat take shape. Accommodation options at Slipper Island Resort provide flexibility, while reliable cellular coverage supports remote work, proving that isolation and connection can coexist with ease.

Life here follows the rhythm of tide and light. Swim in clear waters, kayak quiet bays, fish abundant grounds, and explore the nearby Alderman Islands, a sanctuary for marine life and a place of quiet wonder. Summer grocery deliveries arrive by boat, and the mainland remains close enough when required, yet far enough to truly let go.

This is for the independent thinker. The adventurer. The individual who measures wealth not only in assets, but in experience, privacy, and legacy.

Opportunities of this calibre are seldom offered.

The open-plan rustic kitchen and entertaining deck are ideal for guests or extended family. A gas stove and bar fridge has hosted some fun times. This area also features a flexible sleepout, the spa pool, and flourishing vegetable gardens. The bush and nearby tranquil stream are great for playing music, secluded walks and meditation.

Description:
Step Into A Legacy: Elegant Country Estate Living

Are you ready to elevate your lifestyle and live amidst history, style, and serene beauty? This remarkable country residence is more than just a home - it’s a legacy in the making. Inspired by the iconic shingled rooftops of the Mission House and designed by acclaimed New Zealand architect Robert Railey, this regal estate masterfully blends timeless character with modern comfort.

Entering via the sweeping, Totara-lined driveway, a striking porte-cochere and impressive foyer welcomes you through to two separate lounges and a formal dining room with its own fireplace. Beautiful parquet flooring, a second fireplace and an incredible atrium adjoin the gourmet kitchen - recently renovated with granite benchtops and a new Butler's pantry featuring Tairare milled directly from the property. A separate laundry, plenty of storage, a mud room and triple garaging complete the ground level, along with a double bedroom that - along with the living spaces - opens directly onto a wrap-around patio featuring Hinuera stone.

Upstairs, there are three spacious bedrooms - including a grand master (with ensuite, walk-in robe and a Juliette balcony) and a stunning bathroom with marble tiling - no expense has been spared here.

Outdoor living is catered for via an al fresco dining space (with rain-sensor louvres) alongside the beautiful in-ground pool, expansive lawns and landscaped gardens.

With consent to subdivide into 4 Lots and set on 16.5 hectares of productive soil, mature natives (with resident kiwi), a thriving home orchard - including 300 avocados - and a spring water supply, this estate presents endless possibilities; luxury lifestyle living, boutique B&B potential, high-value crop ventures or simply a peaceful haven for creativity and connection.

Nestled in the heart of Northland’s most historic settlement and just 16km to Kerikeri, opportunities to own a property of this caliber are rare. If you’re ready to step up to a lifestyle defined by beauty, legacy, and potential - your journey begins here. Enquire today.

Outdoors, there’s a substantial orchard featuring a diverse range of trees, including feijoa, olive, avocado, fig, guava, and various citrus varieties, to name just a few. In addition, there are herb gardens, three vegetable gardens complete protective covers. The land is also equipped with five paddocks, ideal for grazing livestock or horses, with the potential for riding in the extended market gardens. URL: https://www.barfoot.co.nz/property/rural/franklin-district/pukekohe/rural-property/895748

Description:
FUTURE URBAN ZONED - HIGH GROWTH AREA

FLEXIBLE SETTLEMENT TERMS AVAILABLE

After many years of being held in the same family this financial growth opportunity is for sale. This brilliant property has elevated views across Pukekohe township and a gentle to rolling contour. It has approximately 325 metres of road frontage along Pukekohe East Road and comprises a total of 39.26 Hectares split between Auckland and Waikato Council. It has a 360 square metre house along with multiple other outbuildings. Location is exceptional, with this site positioned 1.5km from Pukekohe, 50km south of Auckland City, 36km south of the Auckland International Airport and 4km from northern and southern motorway access at Bombay. There is an uncovenanted stand of picturesque native bush, allowing an array of birdlife to flourish.

The Auckland Council portion is 22.05 Hectares Zoned Future Urban while the remaining 17.21 Hectares is zoned Rural under Waikato Regional Council. The Future Urban Zone is a transitional zone applied to greenfield land that has been identified as suitable for urbanisation. Land may be used for a range of general rural activities but cannot be used for urban activities until the site is rezoned for urban purposes. The Pukekohe - Paerata structure Plan 2019 proposes this land to be rezoned to Residential - Mixed Housing Suburban. This plan is estimated to provide about 12,517 houses/dwellings and 5018 jobs with a population of about 33,809. As a comparison, this approximately doubles the existing population of Pukekohe.

Notable plan changes and developments in the area include:

* Anselmi Ridge is 700m to the west with current residential properties and further development underway.

* Kohe Development — 1.2km west of 140 Pukekohe East Road; 30 Hectares of Future Urban Land rezoned to Residential Mixed housing Urban.

* Proposed Private Land Change 98 — Borders the western boundary of 140 Pukekohe East Road and is proposed to rezone 27 hectares of Future Urban land to Mixed Housing Urban Zone.

* Golding Meadows and Auckland Trotting Club Development — 82 hectares re zoned to a combination of Business - Light Industry Zone (19.97ha), Residential - Mixed Housing Urban (62.35ha) and Neighbourhood Centre Zone.

Forward-thinking developers and investors will be quick to recognise the potential this property offers. Whether you land bank it while council rezones it or apply for a private plan change, the potential in this pre-programmed high-growth area is unmatched.

Sale type: For sale by negotiation
Agents: Brendon McCullough : 027578 3899
URL: https://www.barfoot.co.nz/property/rural/tauranga-district/oropi/rural-land/923083

Description:
Where Country Meets Convenience

If you’ve been searching for the perfect balance between peaceful country living and everyday convenience, this is it. Located just 12 minutes from Tauranga’s CBD, this 5044m² (more or less) lifestyle section on 1048C Oropi Road offers the rare opportunity to create a home that truly fits your way of life.

Set on a sunny, flat site, the land provides an easy build platform surrounded by open space and native birdlife — a blank canvas ready for your imagination. Whether you’re dreaming of a modern family retreat or a timeless country home, here you can work with your preferred building partner and bring your vision to life.

There’s room for gardens, fruit trees, or simply the luxury of space to breathe. Wake to the sounds of nature, enjoy outdoor living all year round, and embrace the lifestyle that only Oropi can offer.

Despite its tranquil rural setting, you’re never far from what matters most. Oropi School is just a short drive away, Greerton Village offers shops, cafes, and essential services, and Tauranga Crossing provides supermarkets, restaurants, and retail options. For outdoor lovers, the Oropi Mountain Bike Trail and Otanewainuku bush walks and waterfalls are right on your doorstep — perfect for weekend adventures close to home.

Sections like this don’t come along often — where rural tranquillity meets urban convenience. Make the move and secure your piece of Oropi paradise today.

Title: 97 Mystery Valley Road, Paengaroa
Text:
Location: 97 Mystery Valley Road, Paengaroa
Sale type: For sale by negotiation
Agents: Danny Redwood : 021025 92351
URL: https://www.barfoot.co.nz/property/rural/tauranga-district/paengaroa/horticulture/916943

Description:
Lifestyle, Income and Olive trees.....

Note: Asking Price is Plus GST (if any)

Located within easy commuting distance from Tauranga, Whakatane and Rotorua this exceptional lifestyle property encompasses a small business with potential to expand. There is a minor dwelling with an excellent shed and a lined sleepout currently rented at $500/week, another single bedroom flat and a small beef finishing operation.

The property has a small well-maintained mature olive grove with room for expansion. Olives from the Mystery Valley grove are processed on site by our 250kg/hr Enorossi olive press, in a registered food production facility. We also contract press fruit brought in by other growers. In 2024 and 2025 total tonnage pressed each year has been roughly 20 tonnes, of which 2t.were grown here. The press has consistently produced award winning oils, including oils from the Mystery Valley grove. Mystery Valley olive oil is sold on our website (mysteryvalleyproduce.co.nz) and at Ohope market. There is considerable potential for a motivated operator to expand sales of both oil and the preserves made from fruit grown on site.

The main residence is a warm, beautifully appointed family home—loved and meticulously cared for. Expansive gardens, established fruit trees, chickens, and space for all the pets you desire create an idyllic country lifestyle. A sizable creek borders one boundary, and multiple outbuildings provide versatility for machinery, horses, hobbies, and storage.

Property Features Include:

Equine-ready layout:


Six small paddocks set up for horses, including two stables
Separate wooden tack shed
Large timber barn suitable for horse feed and hay storage

Grazing & Land Use:


Four large grazing paddocks for stock
Two hay paddocks ideal for seasonal feed production

Infrastructure & Outbuildings:


Large wooden-framed two-car garage with workspace
Multiple lean-tos for covered storage
Substantial Coloursteel three-bay shed—one bay certified for food production and currently used for the olive oil pressing operation

Olive Grove:


Approximately 338 established olive trees supporting the boutique oil business

Title: 5 Selwyn Road, Poroporo
Text:
Location: 5 Selwyn Road, Poroporo
Sale type: For sale by negotiation
Agents: Brendon McCullough : 027578 3899
URL: https://www.barfoot.co.nz/property/rural/whakatane-district/poroporo/horticulture/926623

Description:
The Gold Standard

3.72 Canopy Ha Premium Gold Kiwifruit Asset - Proven Production & Infrastructure Excellence.

Rarely does an orchard of this technical specification and efficiency come to market. Comprising 4.77 hectares of total land with a highly efficient 3.72-hectare canopy area of G3 gold, this property is designed for the serious investor or grower looking to scale.

Key Features:
- Variety: Established Gold (G3) kiwifruit in full production.
- Infrastructure: Fully integrated irrigation and fertigation systems for precision crop and vine management.
- Risk Mitigation: Comprehensive frost protection ensures peace of mind during the critical spring budburst.
- Efficiency: A good canopy-to-land ratio, growing over as much of the title as possible.
- Water bore: A must to help mitigate the season changes and ensure control measures when required.

The Investment: With Zespri Gold returns continuing to lead the horticultural sector, this orchard offers a lucrative Orchard Gate Return (OGR) and backed up by strong infrastructure. All the hard work has been done; this is a turnkey operation ready for the 2026 season and carrying a good crop.

Description:
Vendors Loss - Your Gain

This exceptional avocado orchard presents a genuine opportunity to secure a premium horticultural property in a sought-after volcanic location. With motivated vendors and a clear intention to sell, this is your chance to act.

Located approximately 5 minutes from Maungatapere and around 15 minutes from the CBD, the property enjoys a highly desirable position combining rural privacy with everyday convenience.

The orchard features deep, fertile volcanic soil, approximately 1,000 avocado trees, and fully automated irrigation supported by three shares in the Maungatapere Water Company, providing a reliable year-round water supply. North-facing and beautifully contoured to catch the sun, the land offers excellent growing conditions.

Adding further appeal are multiple potential building sites with panoramic rural views, complemented by charming stone walls that enhance the character of the property. Whether you are seeking a quality producing orchard or a premium lifestyle investment, this is a deadline sale not to be missed.
